node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: define `g(x) = f x - f (1 - x)` on `[0,1]`. This `g` is continuous, since `f` is continuous
on `[0,1]` and the map `x -> 1 - x` sends `[0,1]` to itself. We have
`g(0) = f 0 - f 1` and `g(1) = f 1 - f 0 = -g(0)`. If `g(0) = 0`, take `c = 0`. Otherwise `g(0)`
and `g(1)` have opposite signs, so by the intermediate value theorem there exists `c ∈ [0,1]` with
`g(c) = 0`, i.e. `f c = f (1 - c)`.
